Issue: No wrapping <header> or <nav> or <main> landmark elements used. The page uses only <body> with no semantic sectioning elements.
Recommendation: Wrap the page heading and navigation links in <header>, main content in <main>, and add <footer> if applicable.

Issue: No <main> landmark element. All body content is placed directly under <body> without semantic structure.
Recommendation: Wrap the primary content in a <main> element so assistive technologies can identify the main content area.

Issue: Navigation link (About Us) is not wrapped in a <nav> element.
Recommendation: Wrap navigation links in a <nav> element with an aria-label to identify the navigation purpose.

Issue: Image missing alt attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the meaning of this image.
Recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute (e.g., alt="Company logo") or alt="" if decorative.

Issue: innerHTML used with user-controlled input (location.hash). This is an XSS vector and also means content injected this way may not be announced by screen readers.
Recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML for safe text insertion. Sanitize all user input.

Issue: Inline link within paragraph is acceptable but the overall page lacks structural landmarks (header/nav/main/footer).
Recommendation: Ensure the overall page structure uses semantic HTML5 elements.

Issue: Page has only one heading level (<h1>). No heading hierarchy is present for subsections.
Recommendation: Add appropriate heading levels (h2-h6) to create a logical document outline for screen reader navigation.

Issue: No skip-navigation link provided. Keyboard users must tab through all elements to reach main content.
Recommendation: Add a "Skip to main content" link as the first focusable element in the page.

Issue: Link text "About Us" is acceptable but the link has no visual distinction defined in the page itself (relies on external CSS).
Recommendation: Ensure links are distinguishable by more than just color (e.g. underline).

Issue: Image without alt text will be announced as the filename by screen readers which is not meaningful.
Recommendation: Provide a descriptive alt attribute or mark as decorative with alt="" and role="presentation".

Issue: Image missing explicit width and height attributes causes layout shift (CLS) which can disorient users of magnification tools.
Recommendation: Add width and height attributes to prevent layout shifts.

Issue: No use of ARIA landmarks (role=banner/navigation/main/contentinfo) or HTML5 equivalents.
Recommendation: Use HTML5 semantic elements (<header>/<nav>/<main>/<footer>) which implicitly provide ARIA landmark roles.

Issue: lang attribute is set to "en" which is good. No issues here.
Recommendation: No action needed. This is correctly implemented.
